concept: Evening / End of Day / Time of Gathering
description: The period from sunset to bedtime; the day's closing hours. 'Howai' names the time of return—gathering home, sharing meals, the transition from work to rest.
sound_symbolism: The word settles homeward. 'Ho' breathes with day's release, settling complete. 'Wa' flows with gentle transition, light fading. Then 'i' rests into stillness, evening arrived.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'mua howai' (in the evening). Time of community gathering, shared meals, and transition toward rest.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Evening conversation differs from day—slower, more reflective, suited to connection rather than task.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Evening gathered families—the time when scattered members returned, stories were shared, bonds renewed.
